metadata.title: Curry x 21 Savage — Editorial Cut v2
metadata.prompt: Hand-crafted editorial: Steph Curry highlights synced to 21 Savage 'Outfit' with WHY reasoning, lyric sync, and unpredictable pacing
metadata.editorialPhilosophy: The edit follows the lyrical arc, not just the beat. Every effect has a reason tied to either the lyric, the footage action, or the emotional beat. Pacing is intentionally uneven — long breaths between bursts of chaos.

77.80.context: I'm rich, I ain't rent one
77.80.visual: flash on the word, Curry confidence moment

85.74.visual: team energy, group shot if available

100.48.visual: hard cut on the word 'cut' — literal

shots.editorialNote: Establish: wide shot, no effects. Let the audience settle in. The verse hasn't started yet — this is the inhale before the exhale.
First beat hit. 21 says 'I'm rich' at 77.8s — the flash punctuates the word. Curry's first highlight of the verse.
Breathing room. No effects. The verse is building but we hold back — let the footage speak.
Shake on the crossover. The lyric says '10, 9' — counting down. Shake mirrors the rhythmic bounce of the dribble.
Clean cut. 'If she asked for money I sent some' — casual delivery matches casual edit.
Flash on three-pointer release. The ball is in the air — the flash is the anticipation. We don't show the make yet.
Longest shot so far. Three seconds of nothing — just the ball going in and the crowd reacting. This is the exhale. Real editors know when to shut up.
Chromatic on celebration. 'Out of trust, BP we got 10 on' — team energy. The chromatic split mirrors the split-second decision-making on the court.
Clean cut. 'When you talk about real and I been one' — no flash needed. The lyric does the work.
Flash on 'double tap' (95.5s in original). The lyric literally says double tap — we flash once, hard. This is the word-to-visual sync.
Second flash — the 'double' in double tap. Two flashes back to back, then nothing. The unpredictability is the point.
Speed ramp: 0.5× slow-mo. 'Put a switch on the back' — the beat goes half-time here. The slow-mo matches the lyrical switch. We hold for 3 full seconds. Let the hang time breathe.
Speed up to 2× — release of tension. 'Got a double tap' repeats. The chromatic here is the visual equivalent of a bass drop.
Wide shot, no effects. 'Get a smack turn around' — the action speaks. Sometimes the best edit is no edit.
Shake on 'G block cut' (100.5s). The lyric says 'cut' — we shake instead of cutting. Subverting expectation.
Clean cut. 'Throw what they begging at' — Curry's signature. No effects needed. The move IS the effect.
Final flash — 'it's a few rap niggas that I never rap.' Hard stop. The edit ends on the lyric's emphasis. One flash, then blackout.

editorialRules: Effects only when the lyric or action demands it — never decorative
At least 30% of shots must be clean cuts (no effects)
Never repeat the same effect pattern twice in a row
Speed ramps only on half-time beats or lyrical switches
Flash transitions only on impact words (rich, double tap, cut)
Shake on physical action (crossover, dribble, contact)
